rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: GREG E. LINDBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Enforce Subpoena Pursuant To Ccp 2029.600 Et Seq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law and Motion calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2025, Line 1. PETITIONER GREG LINDBERG's Hearing On Petition To Enforce Subpoena Pursuant To Ccp 2029.600 Et Seq. The court denies the petition without prejudice, without reaching its merits. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. TZETZANGARI MORENO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursaunt To Stipulation Of The Parties
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law and Motion calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursaunt To Stipulation Of The Parties. The unopposed motion for entry of judgment is granted in part and denied in part. The parties stipulated to entry of judgment against defendant Tzetzangari Moreno in the amount of $29,888.24, what the stipulation deems the "Judgment Amount." (Ex. 1 to Zarco Dec. at para. 1.) But the stipulation further provided that entry of the judgment would be deferred and Moreno would make payments to Bank of America. (Id. paras. 3-4.) If Moreno failed to make timely payments, Bank of America could seek entry of judgment for "the Judgment Amount plus any motion and/or order fee(s) required by the court, less credit for payments made." (Id. para. 7.) The Zarco declaration shows that Moreno paid $14,000. The fee for filing this motion was $60. Accordingly, the court grants the motion and will enter judgment in the amount of $15,948.24 in favor of Plaintiff. Plaintiff shall prepare a proposed judgment conforming to this order in accordance with Rule of Court 3.1312. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: METROPOLITAN ELECTRICAL CONSTRUCTION, INC, VS. SUTTER HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law and Motion calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2025, Line 3. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. Plaintiff Metropolitan Electrical Construction took the defaults of two non-appearing defendants, Bayview Environmental Services and Bluewater Environmental Services. Those defendants later moved to set aside the defaults. Metropolitan opposed Bayview's motion and did not oppose Bluewater's motion. None of those three parties served courtesy copies of their papers in advance of the hearing, in contravention of San Francisco Local Rule 2.7B. The hearing was originally set for December 27, 2024. The court continued the matter on its own motion to January 28, 2025. The court's minute order stated, "All parties to provide courtesy copies per SF Local Rule 2.7B with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date." Before the January 28, 2025 hearing, Metropolitan did not comply with the court's December 27, 2024 order to provide courtesy copies. The court issued an order to Metropolitan to show cause why it should not be sanctioned $250 for its failure to follow the local rule. Metropolitan now opposes sanctions, arguing that the rule itself does not include sanctions as a consequence. Metropolitan is correct. The rule does not; it is the violation of this court's December 27 minute order that provides the basis for the sanction. (See CCP 177.5 ["A judicial officer shall have the power to impose reasonable money sanctions . . . payable to the court, for any violation of a lawful court order by a person, done without good cause or substantial justification."].) The court's order to show cause was insufficiently precise and should have specified that it was the violation of the December 27 minute order ordering compliance with Rule 2.7B that provided the contemplated basis for sanctions. The court discharges the OSC without sanction, but with a reminder to Metropolitan to adhere carefully to the court's local rules and any court orders. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
